LAW AND MOTION 301, THERE BEING NO TIMELY NOR PROPER CHALLENGE FILED AS TO HON. JUDGE JAMES L. WARREN, PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ' MOTION TO CONTINUE SUMMARY JUDGMENT HEARING WAS HEARD. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION WAS CONTINUED TO 5/18/06 FOR WITNESSES TO APPEAR AND PROVIDE TESTIMONY UNDER OATH AND SUBJECT TO CROSS EXAMINATION. JUDGE JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ' MOTION TO CONTINUE SUMMARY JUDGMENT HEARING WAS DENIED ON THE MERITS. AN EVIDENTIARY HEARING WAS HELD REGARDING SERVICE. SERVICE WAS RULED VALID. EXHIBITS WERE RETURNED TO DEFENDANT PER THE COURT'S VERBAL ORDER. JUDGE JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOE GRUBB, DELENE WOLF, and ROBERT COLLINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION was granted. Burden shifted. No opposition filed. Prevailing party to submit proposed order. (301/JLW) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Plaintiff Maria Martinez'S Motion To Continue Summary Judgment Hearing
Off Calendar May-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Craig K. Martin, Esq., 640-4803, for Plaintiff and Moving Party
Gina M. Roccanova, Deputy City Attorney, 554-4241, for Defendant and Opposing Party
RULING:
PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ' Motion To Continue Summary Judgment Hearing was denied on the merits. An evidentiary hearing was held regarding service. Plaintiff's witness Joesiah Bell was sworn in and examined. Defendant's witnesses Wanda Takao and Sean Raymond were sworn in and examined. Defendant's Exhibit A and B were Marked for Identification and Received into Evidence. Service was ruled valid. PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ' Motion To Continue Summary Judgment Hearing was denied. Exhibits were returned to Defendant per Court's verbal order. (301/JLW) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2006 Sanctions
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Order to Show Cause to Craig K. Martin, Esq., for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is heard this day. Court ordered Plaintiff's counsel sanctioned $250.00, for failure to appear. The OSC is not re-set at this time due to the proximity of the Trial Date. Proceedings reported by: Peppina Thompson, CSR#7433 (212)

Notice Of Motion To Continue Summary Judgment Hearinq
Off Calendar May-17-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Craig K. Martin, Esq., 640-4803, for Plaintiff and Moving Party
Gina M. Roccanova, Deputy City Attorney, 554-4241, for Defendant and Opposing Party
RULING:
There being no timely nor proper challenge filed as to Hon. Judge James L. Warren, PLAINTIFF MARIA MARTINEZ' Motion To Continue Summary Judgment Hearing was heard. Defendant's counsel did not stipulate to waive the filing of a proper CCP 473 Motion, but Plaintiff's Motion was continued to 5/18/06 for Witnesses Shawn Raymond, Jo Bell, and Dianne Bell to appear and provide testimony under oath and subject to cross examination. (301/JLW/dm) Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton, CSR #1558.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2005 Set For Trial On Jun-19-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On May-31-06 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 505

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gina M. Roccanova, appeared for Defendant/Moving party
No other appearance

Ruling:
Hearing held. Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $696, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
